name: Tomato Ketchup
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut tomatoes into quarters and puree them in a food processor along with the bell pepper.
2. Strain the puree through a coarse sieve to remove skins and seeds. (You can dump the puree into a colander and work it through with your hands until there is nothing left in the colander but a dryish pulp of skins and seeds.)
3. Puree onions, combine with tomato and pepper puree, and pour into a large stainless steel or enameled kettle.
4. Cook and stir occasionally over low heat until it is reduced by about a third and is considerably thicker.
5. Meanwhile, put garlic, peppercorns, allspice, cloves, cinnamon, and celery seed into the vinegar in a small pot and simmer covered for 1/2 hour to steep spices in the vinegar.
6. Pour about half the spiced vinegar through a tea strainer into the thickened tomato mixture. Stir.
7. Also add sugar, mustard, cayenne, and salt at this point.
8. Taste and adjust ingredients as desired. Cook some more, stirring often, until it looks like ketchup should look.
9. If it looks slightly curdled, process in a food processor to smooth out.
10. Pour into sterile jars leaving 1/8 inch of head space.
11. Process in a boiling water bath for 15 minutes.
